Speaker: DF
Manx: Err, ny mraane shoh, [?]ta'n enney oc orrym's [?]agglagh
English: Err, these women, they know me awfully well.

Speaker: NM
Manx: Son t'ou, t'ou jeeaghyn cha mie.
English: Because you, you look so well.

Speaker: NM
Manx: Ta, ta, t'ou jeeaghyn feer vie nish, as t'ad ooilley goaill notion jeed.
English: Yes, yes, you look very well now, and they're all taken a notion of you.

Speaker: DF
English: I don't believe that at all!

Speaker: NM
Manx: Ny mraane, ny mraane aeg. Ah, aha, huh. Bee oo, bee oo goit voish yn ben shen t'ou goll marish ayns Doolish.
English: The women, the young women. Ah, aha, huh. You'll be taken by that women you're going with in Douglas.

Speaker: DF
Manx: Ta, oh t'ee ersooyl ec yn chabbal jiu, ghooinney.
English: Yes, oh she's off at the chapel today, man.

Speaker: DF
Manx: T'eh laa, feailley mooar goll er.
English: There's a day, a big festival going on.

Speaker: NM
Manx: Ah well, ta na-, shegin da nane jeu, jiu goll dys y cabbal.
English: Ah well, one of them, of you has to go the chapel.

Speaker: NM
Manx: Cuin v'ou, tra v'ou uss ayns y cabbal?
English: When were you, you in the chapel?

Speaker: DF
Manx: Ah cha nel mee coontey monney jeh'n cabbal.
English: Ah I don't think much of the chapel.

Speaker: WR
Manx: Cha jean eh goll stiagh ayns y cabbal noadyr.
English: He won't go into the chapel at all.

Speaker: NM
Manx: Cha nel? Ah well, ta shen ...

Speaker: DF
Manx: Ta mee goll dys yn cheeill.

Speaker: NM
Manx: Well, oh vel, vel? Oh t'ou uss goll dys yn keeill as t'eshyn, as t'ee ish goll dys y cabbal? Nee shen eh?
English: Well, oh do you, do you? Oh you go to the church and he, she goes to the chapel? Is that it?

Speaker: DF
Manx: Oh ta. Ta. Ish goll dys y ... Ta.

Speaker: DF
Manx: Ta mish goll dys yn ard-cheeill.

Speaker: WR
Manx: V'eh ec yn keeill Jedoonee chaie.
English: He was at church last Sunday.

Speaker: DF
Manx: She bunnys Creestee Raueagh ta mish.
English: Yes I'm nearly a Roman Catholic.

Speaker: NM
Manx: Vel? [laughs] T'ou raad liauyr voish Creestee foast!
English: Are you? [laughs] You're a long way from a Christian yet!

Speaker: WC
English: He had a red [?]neck on him.
Notes: "Redneck" can mean a Catholic in Northern England.

Speaker: DF
Manx: Cha row mee liauyr voish Raue ansh erbee. Foddee ta mee liauyr voish Creestee.
English: I wasn't far from Rome anyway.

Speaker: NM
Manx: [laughs] Cha row [sic] eh feer foddey nish [?]nys [sic= gys] v'ou [sic] goll mygeayrt lesh, lesh red bane ort.
English: [laughs] It wasn't [sic = won't be] very long now until you were [sic=will be] going around with a white thing on you.

Speaker: DF
English: Oh it will be, won't be.

Speaker: DF
Manx: Ta mee laccal dy ve, ta mee laccal atchimagh dy bee saggyrt yn skeerey.
English: I want to be, I want awfully to be a parish priest.

Speaker: DF
Manx: Oh ta. Skeerey, Skeerey, Skylley Creest.

Speaker: DF
Manx: Na- na- na-, nagh beagh oo [sic ?=mee] mie son y, son yn obbyr shen? Saggyrt mie.
English: Wouldn't, wouldn't you [sic =?I] be good for, for that work? a good priest.

Speaker: NM
Manx: Oh ta, oh ta ta, yinnagh oo jannoo mie dy liooar, cha jinnagh eh jannoo dy yannoo ren oo, myr ren oo, ren oo jannoo ayns, ayns, ayns Kirk Michael.
English: Oh yes, oh yes yes, you would do well enough, it wouldn't do to do you did, as you did, you did in, in, in Kirk Michael.

Speaker: NM
English: It wouldn't do (unclear) ... when you were the priest!

Speaker: WR
Manx: Oh bee ram jeeill jeant, jeeill jeant.
English: Oh there will be lots of damage done.

Speaker: DF
Manx: Oh, ta. Va mee caillt ghooinney! Va mee caillt.
English: Oh, yes. I was lost, man! I was lost.

Speaker: NM
Manx: Heh. V'ou caillt yn laa shen! Eh?
English: Heh. You were lost that day! Eh?

Speaker: WR
Manx: V'eh coodit mie dy liooar echey, though.

Speaker: NM
Manx: Va, oh va, va, ren eh geddyn, ren eh geddyn harrish mie dy liooar.
English: Yes, oh yes, yes, he got over it ok.

Speaker: DF
Manx: Va mee lhaih eh as lhaih eh son shiaghtinyn roish shen. As v'eh ooilley ayns y, ayns y (unclear) ...
English: I was reading it and reading it for weeks before that. And it was all in the, in the (unclear) ...

Speaker: NM
Manx: As eisht, as eisht ren oo coayl y boayl.
English: And then, and then you lost the place.

Speaker: DF
Manx: Cre [?]mychione Kione Jiarg, ren eshyn coayl ...
English: What about Red Head, he lost ...

Speaker: WR
Manx: Cha ren eh chyndaa yn duillag harrish.
English: He didn't turn the page over.

Speaker: NM
Manx: Oh (cha) ren eh chyndaa yn duillag?
English: Oh he didn't turn the page?

Speaker: DF
Manx: Va ooilley yn sleih fuirraghtyn er my hon.
English: All the people were waiting for me.

Speaker: NM
Manx: Cha row ad toiggal, cha row ad toiggal eh. V'eh kiart dy liooar.
English: They weren't understanding, they weren't understanding it. It was OK.
